blocks.content: The call ended. The screens went dark.
Kit sat in his apartment in LA — the one he'd finally moved out of the dorms into, a one-bedroom with a secondhand couch and film stills tacked to the wall. He sat with the quiet for a while.
A long, easy quiet. The kind you could only have with someone you'd known your whole life.

She was in her dorm room, the Minnesota winter pressed against the window. She had her knees pulled up to her chest, phone held in both hands the way she did when she wanted to hold onto the conversation.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: Any of it. The move. The figuring it out. You.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: Not you. The version of you that might not let me in.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: I know. I watched you leave it in session eight.
Outside her window, snow was coming down in the slow, patient way that covered everything and made the world quiet. She watched it for a moment.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: I love you too. On purpose.
